capabilities: Establish a standardized procedure for replicating key experimental results to ensure reliability and validity across independent trials.

procedure.action: Prepare replication by documenting all experimental parameters, including input data, environment configuration, and execution scripts, ensuring they match the original setup exactly using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Execute replication by running the experiment in an isolated environment using the documented parameters, ensuring no external variables influence the outcome using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Analyze results by comparing the replicated data against the original results using statistical tests such as t-tests or ANOVA to determine consistency using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Assert replication by ensuring all key metrics fall within a predefined confidence interval, logging any deviations for further investigation by asserting all required fields conform to the schema definition; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field; assert field_validation_pass_rate >= 1.0.

Document the entire replication process, including any discrepancies found, and store in a version-controlled repository for future reference and audit using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Procedure for replicating key experimental results to confirm reliability and validity, ensuring consistent outcomes across independent trials.
title: Experiment Replication Procedure for Key Results

failureConditions: Inconsistent results when replication parameters are inaccurately documented, causing deviations in experimental setup and invalidating comparisons.
Invalid conclusions when statistical tests are not applied correctly, leading to false positives or negatives in result validation.
Loss of auditability when replication process is not documented, causing difficulties in tracing discrepancies or improvements.
metrics: replication by ensuring all key metrics fall within a predefined confidence interval, logging any deviations for further
